title: gh#65: R2.5 — Sweep: narrow PostServiceError + PostLifecycleAbort; remove dead inline logic
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/65.
Backlog section: Candidate 2 — Post Lifecycle aggregate.

## Context

Final PR in Candidate 2. Cleans up the now-unused error shapes and inline validation paths after R2.1–R2.4 deliver the aggregate.

## Scope

Narrowing:
- `packages/api/src/services/post.service.ts`:
  - `PostServiceError` is now only used for concerns the aggregate doesn't cover: tag-not-found in transaction context, profile-not-found at row load. Verify with `rg "new PostServiceError"` and either narrow the class to a simpler `{ kind: 'profile_not_found' | 'tag_not_found' }` shape or delete and replace with two named errors. Editorial call — pick whichever produces cleaner route-handler error mapping.
- `packages/worker/src/post-lifecycle.service.ts`:
  - `PostLifecycleAbort` becomes a thin adapter that wraps a `PostInvariantError` for BullMQ-graceful-skip semantics: `class PostLifecycleAbort extends Error { constructor(public readonly invariant: PostInvariantError) ... }`. The worker's catch block in `publish-worker.ts` already special-cases `PostLifecycleAbort.reason` — update to `PostLifecycleAbort.invariant.kind`.
  - `LifecycleAbortReason` type is deleted; consumers reference `PostInvariantError['kind']` directly.

Deletions:
- Any inline `transitionPost(...)` calls in `post.service.ts` and `post-lifecycle.service.ts` that are now covered by aggregate calls — verify with `rg "transitionPost\("`.
- Any duplicate `LifecyclePost` interface in worker — replaced by `PostState` import from `@sms/shared/post`.
- `EDITABLE_STATES` / `DELETABLE_STATES` imports in `post.service.ts` (now only referenced via aggregate).

Documentation:
- Update the doc comment at the top of `post-lifecycle.service.ts` to describe what it now owns (transaction shape, callback wiring, audit-row inserts, notification ordering) and what it delegates (everything in `@sms/shared/post/aggregate.ts`).

## Out of scope

- Behavior changes — verify with full test suite
- Renaming `post.service.ts` to `post.repository.ts` — editorial call, can be a follow-up

## Acceptance

- `pnpm --filter @sms/api build` and test pass
- `pnpm --filter @sms/worker build` and test pass
- `rg "LifecycleAbortReason"` returns no matches
- `rg "transitionPost\(" packages/api/src packages/worker/src` returns no matches outside the aggregate
- `post-lifecycle.service.ts` LOC drops noticeably (target: ≤ 350 lines, down from 679)
- `post.service.ts` LOC drops (target: ≤ 350 lines, down from 567)

## References

- ADRs: [0003](../blob/main/docs/adr/0003-post-lifecycle-is-pure-functions-not-shared-repository.md), [0004](../blob/main/docs/adr/0004-publish-cycle-orchestration-stays-in-the-worker.md)
- Depends on: #64

history.reasoning: The original evidence paths still exist. The aggregate exists and post.service.ts/post-lifecycle.service.ts no longer contain direct transitionPost(...) calls, but several requested cleanup items are still present: packages/worker/src/post-lifecycle.service.ts still defines LifecycleAbortReason at lines 70-81, PostLifecycleAbort still stores public readonly reason instead of wrapping a PostInvariantError at lines 83-87, LifecyclePost still exists at lines 105-113, and publish-worker.ts still branches on err.reason at lines 211-232. packages/api/src/services/post.service.ts still imports DELETABLE_STATES at line 2 and still has a broad PostServiceError/toPostServiceError mapping at lines 67-90 with many new PostServiceError call sites. LOC targets are also not met: post.service.ts is 568 lines and post-lifecycle.service.ts is 847 lines. Targeted Vitest commands could not execute in this read-only sandbox because Vite tried to write node_modules/.vite-temp and failed with EPERM, but current tests still assert the old PostLifecycleAbort(reason) API in post-lifecycle.test.ts and publish-worker.test.ts.
commands: rg -n "PostServiceError|new PostServiceError|LifecycleAbortReason|PostLifecycleAbort|transitionPost\(|EDITABLE_STATES|DELETABLE_STATES|interface LifecyclePost|type LifecyclePost|PostState" packages/api/src/services/post.service.ts packages/worker/src/post-lifecycle.service.ts packages/worker/src packages/shared/src/post packages/shared/src/post/aggregate.ts
pnpm --filter @sms/worker exec vitest run src/__tests__/post-lifecycle.test.ts -t PostLifecycleAbort

history.reasoning: The original evidence paths still exist. Several cleanup items are now fixed: PostLifecycleAbort wraps a PostInvariantError via invariant at packages/worker/src/post-lifecycle.service.ts:60-64, publish-worker reads err.invariant.kind at packages/worker/src/publish-worker.ts:207-208, LifecycleAbortReason has no matches, LifecyclePost has no matches, EDITABLE_STATES/DELETABLE_STATES are no longer imported by post.service.ts, and transitionPost no longer appears in post.service.ts or post-lifecycle.service.ts. The finding is still not fully resolved because PostServiceError remains wider than the requested profile_not_found/tag_not_found shape: PostServiceErrorKind still includes post_not_found at packages/api/src/services/post.service.ts:63 and new PostServiceError('post_not_found') remains at lines 212, 335, and 376. The broad acceptance check for transitionPost outside the aggregate also still finds current API/worker call sites in queue.service.ts, routes/posts.ts, queue-scanner.ts, and auto-destruct-lifecycle.service.ts. LOC targets are not met: post.service.ts is 579 lines and post-lifecycle.service.ts is 821 lines. Targeted Vitest runs and normal build commands could not complete in this read-only sandbox because Vite/tsc attempted to write .vite-temp or dist files and hit EPERM, but tsc --noEmit passed for both @sms/api and @sms/worker.

pnpm --filter @sms/worker exec vitest run src/__tests__/post-lifecycle.test.ts -t PostLifecycleAbort
pnpm --filter @sms/worker exec vitest run src/__tests__/publish-worker.test.ts -t PostLifecycleAbort

history.reasoning: Original evidence paths still exist, but packages/api/src/services/post.service.ts and packages/worker/src/post-lifecycle.service.ts are now thin re-export shims and the implementation moved to post-crud.service.ts/post-lifecycle.impl.ts. Current code satisfies the finding: PostServiceErrorKind is narrowed to 'profile_not_found' | 'tag_not_found' and new PostServiceError call sites are only those two cases; PostLifecycleAbort wraps a PostInvariantError as invariant and publish-worker reads err.invariant.kind; LifecycleAbortReason and LifecyclePost have no matches; transitionPost appears only in packages/shared/src/post/aggregate.ts; EDITABLE_STATES/DELETABLE_STATES are not imported by the API service; the lifecycle doc comment now describes worker-owned orchestration and aggregate delegation. Type checks passed with pnpm --filter @sms/api exec tsc --noEmit and pnpm --filter @sms/worker exec tsc --noEmit. Targeted Vitest and build commands could not complete because this sandbox is read-only and Vite/tsc attempted to write node_modules/.vite-temp or dist files, failing with EPERM; that is an environment limitation, not evidence the finding remains open.
